questions.statement: In an examination of 180 marks of a subject, 75% marks are allocated for theory and the rest for project work. Veena scored 60% in theory. What percentage of marks must she score in the project to achieve an overall 62% marks in the subject?
Ram and Shyam together can complete a work in 12 days. Shyam alone can complete the work in 15 days. Shyam alone worked for 5 days. After that, how long will Ram alone take to complete the work?
A container has 20% milk and 80% water in it. It is mixed with another sample (in equal quantity) having 80% milk and 20% water. What would be the milk content in the final mixture?
What will a sum of Rs 5000 become in 18 months at a compound interest rate of 20% per annum when the interest is compounded half-yearly?
What smallest positive number should be subtracted from 2 and 3 so that 4 is the third proportion to them?
A number is increased by 10% and the increased number is decreased by 20%. Find the net percentage of change in the original number.

Alloys A and B contain copper and zinc in the ratio 7&nbsp;∶ 8 and 4 <span style="">∶</span>&nbsp;1, respectively. In what ratio should A and B be mixed&nbsp;to obtain a new alloy C containing copper and zinc in the ratio 2 <span style="">∶</span> 1?
The ratio of gold and copper in 60 grams of alloy is 2 ∶ 1. How much more copper should be added to make the ratio 1 ∶ 2?
45% of a number is 180, then find the 1/8th of that number.
<span style="">A truck covers three equal distances of 100 km at the speed of 200 km/hr, 300 km/hr and 600 km/hr. Find the average speed of the truck for the whole journey.</span>
The smallest four-digit number which is divisible by 4, 8, 12 and 16 is:
A mixture contains milk and water in the ratio 5&nbsp;∶ 2. If 4 liters of water is added to the mixture, the ratio becomes 5 ∶ 4. What is the quantity of milk in the given mixture?

<p>DIRECTIONS:&nbsp;What&nbsp;approximate&nbsp;value&nbsp;will&nbsp;come&nbsp;in&nbsp;place&nbsp;of&nbsp;the&nbsp;question mark&nbsp;(?)&nbsp;in&nbsp;the&nbsp;following&nbsp;question?</p>

<p>349.99&nbsp;&times;&nbsp;19.99&nbsp;+&nbsp;?&nbsp;&times;&nbsp;180.01&nbsp;=&nbsp;12000</p>
The value of 16 - 4&nbsp;&times; [4 - {98 &divide; 2 of 7 - (6 - 36 &divide; 6 &times; 2)}] is:
A train travelling at the speed of 66 km/h crosses a 300 m long platform in 24 seconds. How long was the train?
​A total of 57 sweets were distributed among 10 children such that each girl gets 5 sweets and each boy gets 6 sweets. Find the number of girls.
The ratio of the present ages of A and B is 8 ∶ 15. Eight years ago, the ratio of their ages was 6 ∶ 13. What will be the ratio of ages of A and B after 8 years from now?
If the sum of two numbers is 55 and their product is 600, then find the sum and product of the reciprocals of the numbers respectively?
The length and breadth of a rectangle are 10 cm and 20 cm, If the length and&nbsp;<span style="">breadth</span> of the rectangle is decreased by 10% and 20%, find the area of the new rectangle.
What is the value of&nbsp;<span class="math-tex">\(\sqrt{5\sqrt{5\sqrt{5\sqrt{....\infty}}}}\)</span>?
If 15 workers can build a wall in 48 hours, then what will be the number of workers&nbsp;required to do the same work in 30 hours?&nbsp;
The ratio of ages of 2 girls is 7 ∶ 9. One is elder to the other by 8 years. What is the ratio of their age after 4 years between elder one and younger one?
The population of a village is 24000 . If it is expected to decrease by 4 percent the next year, then what will be the population of the village next year?
40 men can complete a work in 15 days. They started working, and after 5 days 10 more men joined them. In how many days will the total work be completed?&nbsp;
What is the greatest number that divides 134, 159, and 184 &amp; leaves a remainder 9?&nbsp;
